title: arnica discoidea var. alata
intro: Arnica discoidea var. alata, also known as mountain arnica, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of mountain arnica include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other musculoskeletal conditions. Mountain arnica can be prepared as a herbal remedy through the use of tinctures, ointments, salves, and infusions, which can be applied topically to the affected area for therapeutic relief.
